commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow repair can address the shared-client scoped-store ownership bug without a product decision.; Needs attention: The diff has a concrete credential-routing concern because scoped OAuth refresh state can be captured by a reused shared client.; Review finding: [P1] Separate scoped stores before reusing shared clients — extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts:259 (sha=5c49855b7be4e575eeb14ee2f6a833233528cbbf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds shared Codex app-server OAuth refresh handling, forwards scoped auth stores through direct and startup shared-client paths, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +98. Total +120 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows bound Codex conversations use leased shared clients, current main lacks a shared refresh handler, and upstream Codex requires an `accessToken` response; I did not rerun live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can address the shared-client scoped-store ownership bug without a product decision.
